title: Machinists
description: Set up and operate a variety of machine tools to produce precision parts and instruments out of metal. Includes precision instrument makers who fabricate, modify, or repair mechanical instruments. May also fabricate and modify parts to make or repair machine tools or maintain industrial machines, applying knowledge of mechanics, mathematics, metal properties, layout, and machining procedures.

tasks.text: Calculate dimensions or tolerances, using instruments, such as micrometers or vernier calipers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Machine parts to specifications, using machine tools, such as lathes, milling machines, shapers, or grinders.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Measure, examine, or test completed units to check for defects and ensure conformance to specifications, using precision instruments, such as micrometers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Set up, adjust, or operate basic or specialized machine tools used to perform precision machining operations.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Program computers or electronic instruments, such as numerically controlled machine tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Study sample parts, blueprints, drawings, or engineering information to determine methods or sequences of operations needed to fabricate products.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Monitor the feed and speed of machines during the machining process.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Maintain machine tools in proper operational condition.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Fit and assemble parts to make or repair machine tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Align and secure holding fixtures, cutting tools, attachments, accessories, or materials onto machines.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Confer with numerical control programmers to check and ensure that new programs or machinery will function properly and that output will meet specifications.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Operate equipment to verify operational efficiency.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Evaluate machining procedures and recommend changes or modifications for improved efficiency or adaptability.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Diagnose machine tool malfunctions to determine need for adjustments or repairs.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Design fixtures, tooling, or experimental parts to meet special engineering needs.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Dispose of scrap or waste material in accordance with company policies and environmental regulations.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Confer with engineering, supervisory, or manufacturing personnel to exchange technical information.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Lay out, measure, and mark metal stock to display placement of cuts.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Separate scrap waste and related materials for reuse, recycling, or disposal.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Check work pieces to ensure that they are properly lubricated or cooled.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Support metalworking projects from planning and fabrication through assembly, inspection, and testing, using knowledge of machine functions, metal properties, and mathematics.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Install repaired parts into equipment or install new equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Dismantle machines or equipment, using hand tools or power tools to examine parts for defects and replace defective parts where needed.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Test experimental models under simulated operating conditions, for purposes such as development, standardization, or feasibility of design.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Set up or operate metalworking, brazing, heat-treating, welding, or cutting equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Prepare working sketches for the illustration of product appearance.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Establish work procedures for fabricating new structural products, using a variety of metalworking machines.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Install experimental parts or assemblies, such as hydraulic systems, electrical wiring, lubricants, or batteries into machines or mechanisms.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Advise clients about the materials being used for finished products.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
